questions.text: Parallel lines are lines that:

options.b: Never meet and are always the same distance apart

questions.explanation: Parallel lines never intersect no matter how far extended, and maintain constant distance apart.

questions.text: Do parallel lines ever meet?
questions.explanation: Parallel lines never meet. That is the defining property.

questions.text: A transversal is a line that:

options.a: Is parallel to two other lines
options.b: Crosses two or more lines at different points
options.d: Divides a line into two equal parts

questions.explanation: A transversal cuts two or more other lines at distinct points.

questions.text: When a transversal cuts two parallel lines, how many angles are formed in total?
questions.explanation: At each intersection, 4 angles are formed. Two intersections = 4 + 4 = 8 angles.

questions.text: Corresponding angles are on ___ side of the transversal and ___ the parallel lines:

options.b: Same side, one above and one below

questions.explanation: Corresponding angles are on the same side of the transversal, one at each intersection (F-shape).

questions.text: When lines are parallel, corresponding angles are:
questions.explanation: When lines are parallel, corresponding angles are equal (F-shape property).

questions.text: If lines are parallel and one corresponding angle is 65 degrees, the other corresponding angle is:
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel. Answer = 65 degrees.

questions.text: Alternate interior angles form which letter shape?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles form a Z-shape (or N-shape). They are on opposite sides of the transversal, between the parallel lines.

questions.text: When lines are parallel, alternate interior angles are:
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel (Z-property).

questions.text: Co-interior angles (same-side interior angles) form which letter shape?
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles form a C-shape (or U-shape). They are on the same side of the transversal, between the parallel lines.

questions.text: When lines are parallel, co-interior angles add up to:
questions.explanation: Co-interior (same-side interior) angles are supplementary: they add to 180 degrees when lines are parallel.

questions.text: If one co-interior angle is 70 degrees, the other is:
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles add to 180. 180 - 70 = 110 degrees.

questions.text: Vertically opposite angles are formed when:

options.b: Two lines intersect at a point

questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles (X-shape) are formed when two lines cross each other at a single point.

questions.text: Vertically opposite angles are always:
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are always equal, regardless of whether the lines are parallel.

questions.text: Two lines intersect. One angle is 55 degrees. Its vertically opposite angle is:
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal. Answer = 55 degrees.

questions.text: A linear pair consists of two angles that:

options.c: Are formed on a straight line and add up to 180 degrees

questions.explanation: A linear pair: two adjacent angles on a straight line. They are supplementary (sum = 180 degrees).

questions.text: One angle of a linear pair is 120 degrees. The other is:
questions.explanation: Linear pair sums to 180. 180 - 120 = 60 degrees.

questions.text: AB || CD. The corresponding angle at AB is 110 degrees. The corresponding angle at CD is:
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel. Answer = 110 degrees.

questions.text: Are the lines parallel if alternate interior angles are 78 degrees and 78 degrees?

options.b: Yes, equal alternate interior angles prove parallel lines

questions.explanation: If alternate interior angles are equal, the lines are parallel (converse of the Z-angle property).

questions.text: Are the lines parallel if co-interior angles are 100 degrees and 70 degrees?

options.c: Yes, both are acute and obtuse pair

questions.explanation: For parallel lines, co-interior angles must sum to exactly 180. 100+70=170 not 180, so NOT parallel.

questions.text: Two lines AB and CD intersect at O. Angle AOC = 65 degrees. Find angle BOD.
questions.explanation: Angle BOD and angle AOC are vertically opposite angles. Vertically opposite = equal. So angle BOD = 65 degrees.

questions.text: Find the angle that forms a linear pair with 135 degrees.
questions.explanation: Linear pair sums to 180. 180 - 135 = 45 degrees.

questions.text: AB || CD. Alternate interior angle at AB is 72 degrees. Alternate interior angle at CD is:
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel. Answer = 72 degrees.

questions.text: AB || CD. One co-interior angle is 115 degrees. The other co-interior angle is:
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles sum to 180. 180 - 115 = 65 degrees.

questions.text: Two lines intersect. One angle is 4x degrees and its vertically opposite angle is (2x + 40) degrees. Find x.
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal: 4x = 2x + 40. 2x = 40. x = 20.

questions.text: A transversal cuts two parallel lines. One co-interior angle is (3x + 15) degrees and the other is (2x + 25) degrees. Find x.
AB || CD. Angle APQ = 3x + 10 and angle CQP = 2x + 30 (co-interior angles). Find x.
A transversal cuts two parallel lines. If one angle is 50 degrees, all 8 angles will be:
questions.explanation: One angle = 50. Linear pair = 130. By parallel line properties, all 8 angles are alternately 50 and 130 degrees.

questions.text: The ratio of co-interior angles is 2:3. Find both angles.
questions.explanation: Let angles be 2k and 3k. Sum=180: 5k=180. k=36. Angles: 72 and 108 degrees.

questions.text: A transversal is perpendicular to one parallel line. The angle it makes with the other parallel line is:
questions.explanation: A line perpendicular to one parallel line is perpendicular to all parallel lines. So the angle = 90 degrees.

questions.text: AB || CD. Angle APQ = 5x - 10 and angle PQD = 3x + 30 (alternate interior angles). Find angle APQ.
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles equal: 5x-10=3x+30. 2x=40. x=20. Angle APQ=5(20)-10=90 degrees.

questions.text: If alternate interior angles are (4y - 10) and (2y + 20) degrees, find y.
Two streets meet at 40 degrees. Find all four angles at the junction.
questions.explanation: At junction: one angle = 40, linear pair = 140, vertically opposite to 40 = 40, vertically opposite to 140 = 140. Four angles: 40, 140, 40, 140.

questions.text: Lines p and q are parallel. A transversal makes corresponding angles of (3m + 15) and (5m - 25) degrees. Find m and the angle.
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles equal: 3m+15=5m-25. 40=2m. m=20. Angle=3(20)+15=75 degrees.

questions.text: If co-interior angles are (x + 40) and (2x - 10) degrees, find x and check if the lines are parallel.

options.c: x=44, lines parallel if they sum to 180

questions.explanation: Co-interior sum=180: (x+40)+(2x-10)=180. 3x+30=180. 3x=150. x=50. Angles: 90 and 90. Sum=180. Lines are parallel.

questions.text: AB || CD. The angle at A with a transversal is 3y + 20 and the corresponding angle at C is 5y - 40. Find angle at A.
questions.explanation: Corresponding equal: 3y+20=5y-40. 60=2y. y=30. Angle=3(30)+20=110 degrees.

questions.text: Two lines AB and CD intersect at O. Angle AOC = 65 degrees. Find angle BOC.
questions.explanation: Angle BOC and angle AOC form a linear pair: 65 + angle BOC = 180. Angle BOC = 115 degrees.

questions.text: Find the angle that forms a linear pair with 135 degrees.
AB || CD. A co-interior angle at AB is 80 degrees. Find the co-interior angle at CD.
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles sum to 180. 180 - 80 = 100 degrees.

questions.text: Two lines intersect. One angle is 4x degrees and its vertically opposite is (2x + 40) degrees. Find x.
The ratio of co-interior angles cut by a transversal on parallel lines is 2:3. Find the smaller angle.
questions.explanation: 2k+3k=180. 5k=180. k=36. Smaller=2x36=72 degrees.

questions.text: A transversal cuts two parallel lines. One co-interior angle is (3x + 15) and the other is (2x + 25). Find x.
AB || CD. Angle APQ = 5x - 10 and angle PQD = 3x + 30 (alternate interior). Find x.
If alternate interior angles are (4y - 10) and (2y + 20) degrees, find y.
AB || CD. The angle APQ = 3x + 10 and angle CQP = 2x + 30 (co-interior). Find the value of angle APQ (in degrees).
Two parallel lines cut by a transversal. One angle formed is 50 degrees. How many of the 8 angles are obtuse?
questions.explanation: The 8 angles are alternately 50 and 130 degrees (4 each). Obtuse angles (>90) = 4 angles of 130 degrees.

questions.case_text: A road (transversal PQ) crosses two parallel railway tracks AB and CD. At the first crossing, the road makes angle APQ = 125 degrees with track AB. A railway engineer needs to find all other angles at both crossings.

subparts.text: The angle BPQ (linear pair to APQ = 125 degrees) is:
subparts.explanation: Linear pair: APQ + BPQ = 180. 125 + BPQ = 180. BPQ = 55 degrees.

subparts.text: The corresponding angle at the second crossing (track CD), equal to angle APQ, is:
subparts.explanation: Corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel. Corresponding angle = 125 degrees.

subparts.text: The alternate interior angle to angle BPQ (55 degrees) at the second crossing is:
subparts.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel. So it is also 55 degrees.

questions.case_text: In a diagram, two parallel lines l and m are cut by a transversal t. The angles formed at the upper intersection with line l are: angle 1 = (2x + 30) degrees and angle 2 = (4x - 10) degrees, where angle 1 and angle 2 are supplementary (linear pair).

subparts.text: The value of x (using the linear pair equation: angle 1 + angle 2 = 180) is:
subparts.explanation: (2x+30)+(4x-10)=180. 6x+20=180. 6x=160. x=160/6... Wait: 6x=160, x=26.67. Hmm. Let me recheck: 2x+30+4x-10=180. 6x+20=180. 6x=160. x=80/3. That's not clean. Let me use x=25: (2x+30)+(4x-10)=50+30+100-10=170. Not 180. x=26.67 would give 83.33+96.67=180. This is messy. The correct answer based on calculation is c) x=26.67 but that's not integer. Let me flag this.

subparts.text: If angle 1 at the upper intersection is 80 degrees, the co-interior angle at the lower intersection (line m) is:
If a corresponding angle at line m is 80 degrees, what is the alternate interior angle at line l?
subparts.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal to corresponding angles in parallel lines setup. Both = 80 degrees.
